era_context: Late Old Kingdom Egypt (6th Dynasty), Akkad Empire under Naram-Sin, mature Harappan civilization. This is a period of relative stability in three major civilizations, though environmental stress is beginning to accumulate. The 22nd century BCE crisis (climate change, drought, political collapse) is still 50+ years away.

events.title: Pepi II's reign (continued)
events.description: Pepi II continues his reign in Egypt. The Old Kingdom is in its final phase. No specific events are recorded for this year. The broader context is one of gradual decentralization and the weakening of pharaonic authority.
events.certainty_note: Pepi II's reign is traditionally dated c. 2278–2184 BCE. This year falls within that range, but absolute dating is uncertain by ±50–100 years.
cross_references: Continuation of earlier Pepi II entries

events.title: Naram-Sin's reign (continued)
events.description: Naram-Sin continues to rule the Akkad Empire. No specific events are recorded for this year. The empire remains at its height.
events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is dated c. 2254–2218 BCE. This year falls within that range.

events.title: Harappan civilization (continued)
events.description: The Indus Valley Civilization continues at its height. No specific events are recorded for this year.
events.certainty_note: The Harappan civilization's existence and chronology are confirmed by archaeology.

disconfirming_evidence: No new disconfirming evidence specific to this year.
historiographic_note: This year is undocumented in terms of specific events. The 'history' presented is inference from broader chronological frameworks.
